meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between code and design. Learn how to use Git with AI, Antigravity, and Claude Code to deliver professional-grade UI without design skills.
content.answer_block: You don't need a designer to ship high-conversion apps. By integrating Git-based AI workflows with Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative UI refinement, you turn natural language into production-ready frontend code. This pipeline allows you to prototype, iterate, and deploy professional UI components in record time.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the client’s deadline is looming, and your design skills are, at best, 'functional.' The gap between a robust backend and a polished frontend is where most freelance projects die. You know the logic, but the aesthetics feel like an insurmountable wall that keeps you from scaling your freelance business.

Stop fighting the design barrier. By leveraging the synthesis of Git-based versioning and AI-driven UI generation, you can effectively outsource the 'visual intuition' to agents. We’re moving beyond drag-and-drop builders; we’re moving into a workflow where your code repository becomes a living, breathing design system that evolves with every prompt.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Establish a clean repository structure that allows AI agents to traverse your file system without getting lost in legacy clutter.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a modern React/Tailwind project structure with a focus on modular component architecture. Ensure all folders are correctly mapped for AI context injection.

steps.title: Define the Design System
steps.description: Use AI to define your CSS variables and theme configuration before writing a single line of component code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a cohesive Tailwind config and global.css file based on a 'modern SaaS' aesthetic. Include primary, secondary, and error color palettes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply the generated tailwind config to the project root'

steps.title: Component Scaffolding
steps.description: Generate UI components by describing their function rather than their styling. Let the AI handle the layout and responsiveness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive landing page Hero component using Shadcn UI primitives. Focus on mobile-first layout and clean typography.

steps.title: AI-Driven UI Iteration
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform 'visual diffing' by feeding it screenshots of your current build to refine spacing and alignment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current UI screenshot and suggest specific CSS adjustments to improve visual hierarchy and whitespace.
steps.claude_code_command: claude observe --screenshot ./preview.png --task 'fix spacing and padding issues'

steps.title: Automated Code Review & PR
steps.description: Automate the Git workflow by having your agent verify the UI integrity before committing to the main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for accessibility compliance and responsiveness. Create a summary of changes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit -m 'feat: implement refined UI components via AI feedback'

pro_tips: Always use a 'design system' prompt as your context file to ensure AI consistency across different pages.
Use Claude Code's '--diff' flag to review AI-suggested UI changes before applying them to your production branch.
Feed your client's brand guidelines as a text file into the Antigravity context to force the AI to align with their specific visual language.
Implement a 'preview-to-prompt' loop where you screenshot the UI, feed it to Claude, and ask for specific design critiques.
Use Git tags to mark 'Design Milestones' so you can instantly revert to a previous aesthetic if an AI refactor goes off-brand.
content.next_steps: Begin by auditing your current project's design system. Once you have a base theme, move to component-level generation. Focus on building a library of reusable UI patterns so your next project is 80% pre-built.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design limitations dictate your freelance revenue. Master the tools that turn you from a coder into a full-stack product architect. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
